keyRules: Up to 120 m above the ground (EASA Open Category)
Register as a UAS operator if the drone weighs 250 g or more or is equipped with a camera
A1/A3 online training is mandatory — minimum age 16
Mandatory liability insurance for ALL drone weights (coverage of €1–2.5 million)
Brussels: dual permits required (federal + regional approval)
Always maintain visual line of sight (VLOS) of the drone
Do not fly over assemblies of people. Rules for uninvolved persons depend on subcategory, class mark and operating conditions; check the official matrix.
At least 3 km away from airports and heliports
Night flight is permitted under the EASA open-category baseline when the required green flashing light is used, unless a current geographic zone or national rule imposes a stricter condition.
Check Droneguide for zone-specific altitude limits and restrictions
importantNotes: Mandatory insurance for ALL drone weights — coverage of €1–2.5 million required
Brussels requires both federal registration AND regional authorization
Three airports (Brussels, Charleroi, Liège) are establishing large restricted zones
Fines and criminal prosecution may result from violations
The GDPR applies when creating images that depict identifiable individuals

fields.disclaimerText: Always check the official BCAA sources and Droneguide before flying. This app is a tool—not a legal authority. Belgium follows EASA regulations, which include strict national insurance requirements.
